message:
fix(mux/domain): flatpak shell-lookup must fail loudly on non-zero exit or empty $SHELL

Pivoted from /testing-fuzzing on frankenterm/async_ossl/ to
/multi-pass-bug-hunting on frankenterm/mux/src/domain.rs. async_ossl is
a 448-line I/O adapter around openssl::ssl::SslStream<TcpStream> with
no pure state-machine surface — construction needs live TCP + cert
material, so it has no meaningful proptest fuzz target.

Three-pass audit of frankenterm/mux/src/domain.rs:

  1. unwrap/expect on fallible paths: clean. Every .unwrap / .expect
     site in the prod zone (lines 248, 261, 275, 311, 510, 522, 657,
     768) is an `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
     fallback with no panicking path.

  2. locks across await: clean. All parking_lot::Mutex lock() call
     sites (lines 349, 560, 564, 576, 579, 582, 585, 652) are
     synchronous parking_lot locks scoped to a single expression.
     None bridges a .await.

  3. input validation / error-path visibility: defect found and fixed.

The flatpak-sandbox branch of LocalDomain::fixup_command at
domain.rs:453-463 shells out to the host via `flatpak-spawn --host sh
-c 'echo $SHELL'` to learn the host-side default shell (the sandbox
can't read $SHELL directly). The earlier code swallowed two failure
modes:

  * non-zero exit from flatpak-spawn — missing host socket, --host
    denied by policy, the helper not on PATH — was dropped on the
    floor; the code still pushed `String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.
    stdout).trim().to_string()` (often empty) as the shell argv entry,
    producing a malformed `flatpak-spawn` argv whose downstream
    `spawn_command` failure carried no context pointing at the real
    cause. The user's new pane would just say
    "command not found" with no hint the real problem was a flatpak
    policy issue upstream.

  * empty $SHELL on the host — `echo $SHELL` legitimately returns a
    bare newline when the host shell has no SHELL set, in which case
    the old code pushed `""` as a program name and `exec()` failed
    with an opaque ENOENT.

Fix (domain.rs around line 453):

  * add `.context("invoking flatpak-spawn --host sh -c 'echo $SHELL'")`
    on the output() call so an I/O failure carries a clear upstream
    label;
  * check `output.status.success()` and bail with the exit code +
    stderr tail if flatpak-spawn failed;
  * trim stdout and bail with a user-actionable message if the shell
    string is empty, suggesting either setting SHELL on the host or
    spawning an explicit program.

Both failure paths now propagate as `anyhow::Error` up through
`fixup_command → build_command → spawn_pane`, where `spawn_pane`'s
existing `write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok()` block (domain.rs:693) will
render the labeled context inside the new pane so the user actually
sees what went wrong.

Compile-verified: `cargo check --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo
.toml` finishes clean in 1m 36s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
